2605.04901.title: On the (In-)Security of the Shuffling Defense in the Transformer Secure Inference
2605.04901.paper_summary: For Transformer models, cryptographically secure inference ensures that the client learns only the final output, while the server learns nothing about the client's input. However, securely computing nonlinear layers remains a major efficiency bottleneck due to the substantial communication rounds and data transmission required. To address this issue, prior works reveal intermediate activations to the client, allowing nonlinear operations to be computed in plaintext. Although this approach significantly improves efficiency, exposing activations enables adversaries to extract model weights. To mitigate this risk, existing works employ a shuffling defense that reveals only randomly permuted activations to the client. In this work, we show that the shuffling defense is not as robust as previously claimed. We propose an attack that aligns differently shuffled activations to a common permutation and subsequently exploits them to extract model weights. Experiments on Pythia-70m and GPT-2 demonstrate that the proposed attack can align shuffled activations with mean squared errors ranging from $10^{-9}$ to $10^{-6}$. With a query cost of approximately \$1, the adversary can recover model weights with L1-norm differences ranging from $10^{-4}$ to $10^{-2}$ compared to the oracle weights.
2605.04901.paper_summary_zh: 對於Transformer模型，密碼學安全推斷確保客戶端僅學習最終輸出，而伺服器對客戶端的輸入一無所知。<lb/>然而，安全計算非線性層仍然是一個主要的效率瓶頸，因為需要大量的通信輪次和數據傳輸。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，先前的研究向客戶端揭示中間激活，允許在明文中計算非線性操作。<lb/>雖然這種方法顯著提高了效率，但暴露激活使對手能夠提取模型權重。<lb/>為了減輕這一風險，現有的研究採用了隨機置換的防禦措施，僅向客戶端揭示隨機排列的激活。<lb/>在本研究中，我們表明隨機置換防禦並不像先前聲稱的那樣穩健。<lb/>我們提出了一種攻擊，將不同隨機排列的激活對齊到一個共同的置換，並隨後利用它們來提取模型權重。<lb/>在Pythia-70m和GPT-2上的實驗表明，所提出的攻擊可以將隨機排列的激活對齊，均方誤差範圍從$10^{-9}$到$10^{-6}$。<lb/>以約\$1的查詢成本，對手可以恢復模型權重，L1範數差異範圍從$10^{-4}$到$10^{-2}$，與oracle權重相比。
2605.04901.author: Zhengyi Li et.al.
2605.04901.authors: Zhengyi Li, Yakai Wang, Kang Yang, Yu Yu, Jiaping Gui, Yu Feng, Ning Liu, Minyi Guo, Jingwen Leng
